workspace.description: Rust MCP server for bounded code intelligence, gated mutation, and auditable agent workflows

modes.name: solo-local
modes.purpose: Single-agent local work without cross-agent coordination overhead.
modes.best_fit: One editor or terminal session exploring and editing the repository directly.
modes.mutation_policy: same session can plan and edit; refactor-full still requires verifier evidence before mutation

roles.responsibility: one session handles both planning and implementation

modes.name: planner-builder
modes.purpose: Primary multi-agent pattern: read-only planning/review paired with mutation-enabled implementation.
modes.best_fit: Claude planning/review plus Codex building, or any equivalent planner/builder split.
modes.mutation_policy: exactly one mutation-enabled agent per worktree; planners stay read-only

roles.responsibility: bootstrap, rank context, and verify change readiness before dispatch
execute bounded edits after preflight, diagnostics, and claims

modes.name: reviewer-gate
modes.purpose: Read-only signoff lane that checks builder output before merge or handoff.
modes.best_fit: PR review, risk signoff, CI-facing structural review, or planner validation after a builder run.
modes.mutation_policy: no content mutation; fail the session audit if mutation traces appear

roles.responsibility: diff-aware review, impact analysis, and audit signoff

recommended_flow: audit_builder_session if reviewing a prior builder session
recommended_audits: audit_builder_session for the session under review

modes.name: batch-analysis
modes.purpose: Asynchronous analysis lane for repo-wide or long-running read-side jobs.
modes.best_fit: Dead-code sweeps, architecture scans, semantic review queues, and non-interactive evaluation passes.
modes.mutation_policy: read-only; use analysis handles and job artifacts rather than direct edits

roles.responsibility: start durable jobs and consume bounded sections instead of raw full reports

recommended_audits: audit_planner_session when the run stayed on planner/reviewer surfaces

routing_policy.reason: CodeLens adds bootstrap and protocol overhead that is not worth paying for trivial point lookups, but wins when bounded workflow evidence, session-scoped audit, or reusable artifacts matter.

contracts.name: planner-builder-handoff
contracts.intent: Planner/reviewer session prepares bounded evidence, then a mutation-enabled builder session executes the change under explicit coordination.

roles.responsibility: collect structure, diagnostics, and readiness evidence before dispatch
perform bounded mutation only after preflight, diagnostics, and coordination

preflight_sequence.purpose: establish session-local project view, visible surface, and health summary
record structural evidence for the touched files
record baseline diagnostic evidence for the touched files

preflight_sequence.when: once for the full change set before mutation
preflight_sequence.purpose: produce readiness status, blockers, and overlapping claim evidence

coordination_discipline.required_for: non-local-http builder sessions that mutate files

steps.purpose: publish session identity, worktree, branch, and intent
publish advisory file reservations for the intended change set
explicitly release claims instead of waiting for TTL expiry

notes: run post-edit diagnostics after the mutation pass
builder audit stays audit-only and does not add new runtime hard blocks

gates.reason: builder mutation must not start while the verifier reports blockers
the orchestrator decides whether to wait, reassign, or continue
rename_symbol requires symbol-aware evidence, not only generic readiness

example.task: Implement the bounded change described by the planner

contracts.name: reviewer-signoff
contracts.intent: Read-only reviewer or CI-facing session validates a builder session and exports a human-readable signoff artifact.

roles.responsibility: perform diff-aware review, signoff, and audit validation without content mutation

read_sequence.purpose: bind the reviewer session to the project and bounded read-side surface
collect diff-aware and impact-aware evidence for the change under review
validate read-side bootstrap, workflow-first routing, and file evidence discipline
validate the paired builder/refactor session before merge or handoff
emit a human-readable reviewer or builder audit summary

gates.condition: workflow is diff-aware but target paths are missing
gates.reason: review_changes, impact_report, and related workflows require change evidence

contracts.name: batch-analysis-artifact
contracts.intent: Long-running read-only analyses should move through durable jobs and bounded sections rather than raw full-report expansion.

roles.responsibility: queue durable read-side jobs and consume bounded sections

analysis_sequence.purpose: establish the analysis surface and runtime health view
create a durable analysis job and handle
track job state without reopening a raw report

analysis_sequence.when: to expand only one section at a time
analysis_sequence.purpose: keep the analysis bounded and section-oriented

gates.condition: analysis requires full raw report expansion before a handle exists
gates.reason: batch-analysis should stay handle-first and section-oriented

host_adapters.goal: Adapt CodeLens usage to the host's native agent model instead of forcing one universal harness shape everywhere.

root_causes.problem: Routing decisions live in chat memory, personal habit, or repo-local folklore instead of a portable product contract.
root_causes.effect: Other repositories repeat bootstrap overhead, skip useful audits, or misuse CodeLens on trivial point edits.

root_causes.problem: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and similar hosts expose different primitives for subagents, worktrees, rules, background execution, and MCP governance.
root_causes.effect: A one-size-fits-all harness either underuses native host strengths or leaks too much surface into the wrong execution path.

root_causes.problem: Shared infrastructure is asked to own host UI behavior, live agent chat, and orchestration policy at the same time.
root_causes.effect: Control-plane complexity grows faster than measurable value.

root_causes.problem: New routing lanes, skills, or adapters are added without ground-truth data or a merge-gating signal.
root_causes.effect: The harness bloats while quality remains unproven.

design_principles: Keep CodeLens as the durable substrate for session state, audit, handoff, and bounded workflow tools.
Treat host-specific behavior as an adapter/compiler concern, not as a reason to fork the substrate.
Prefer asymmetric handoff and role-specialized surfaces over always-on live multi-agent chat.
Escalate from native host tools to CodeLens when the task becomes multi-file, reviewer-heavy, refactor-sensitive, or artifact-worthy.
Ship only evaluation lanes that add new signal beyond existing audits or benchmark gates.

owned_by_codelens: portable handoff schema and runtime resources

detection_inputs: task phase (lookup, plan, review, build, eval)
need for durable artifacts or session audit
routing_outputs: whether analysis jobs should replace direct long reports

delegate_scaffold_contract.replay_rule: preserve delegate_tool, delegate_arguments, carry_forward, and handoff_id verbatim for the first delegated builder call

hosts.name: claude-code
hosts.best_fit: planner and reviewer orchestration with isolated research and explicit policy control
preferred_codelens_use: planner-session audit and handoff artifact production
avoid: defaulting to live bidirectional chat between planner and builder
exposing mutation-heavy surfaces to read-side sessions

hosts.name: codex
hosts.best_fit: builder and refactor execution, parallel worktree-based implementation, and automation
avoid: forcing CodeLens into trivial single-file lookups
copying Claude-specific subagent topology into Codex worktree flows

hosts.name: cursor
hosts.best_fit: editor-local iteration with scoped rules plus asynchronous remote execution when needed
preferred_codelens_use: minimal surface exposure through mode- or rule-specific routing
avoid: assuming foreground and background agents share the same trust boundary
shipping the full CodeLens surface into every mode

hosts.name: cline
hosts.best_fit: human-in-the-loop debugging and foreground execution with explicit approvals
preferred_codelens_use: session audit and handoff artifacts when a change must cross sessions
avoid: treating Cline as a headless CI runner
relying on CodeLens where the foreground checkpoint loop already provides the needed safety

hosts.name: windsurf
hosts.best_fit: editor-local implementation with a hard MCP tool cap and bounded foreground agent flows
preferred_codelens_use: bounded builder execution under a small visible surface
compressed planning when the task escapes single-file scope
avoid: attaching the full CodeLens surface alongside many other MCP servers
using reviewer-heavy profiles as the default editing surface

agent_experience.goal: Expose a portable product and agent-flow contract that both humans and agent hosts can consume directly.

naming_policy.rule: Keep the public install/docs/binary name CodeLens-first. Runtime aliases may remain for compatibility, but outward-facing product language stays CodeLens.

core_surfaces.purpose: Install, verify, and attach the MCP server to a host in under one minute.
Show active profile, visible surface, health, and current session scope.
Translate task phase and risk into role profile, preferred executor, and next-tool shortlist.
Summarize bootstrap, verifier, mutation, and signoff evidence per session.
Inspect planner/builder/reviewer artifacts and synthetic delegation scaffolds without reading raw chat history.
Remove or migrate the attachment cleanly without residue.

delegation_contract.replay_rule: preserve delegate_tool, delegate_arguments, carry_forward, and handoff_id verbatim for the first delegated builder call
delegation_contract.correlation_rule: hosts should replay delegate_arguments.handoff_id unchanged so planner-side delegate emission and builder-side execution can be correlated across sessions

delegate_scaffold: treat delegate_to_codex_builder as synthetic advisory host action, not a server-callable tool
do not reconstruct delegated builder arguments from prose when delegate_arguments are present
preserve handoff_id at the scaffold top level and inside delegate_arguments for the first builder-heavy call
session_closeout: use export_session_markdown(session_id=...) as the canonical per-session artifact source
keep eval_session_audit as a runtime-scoped aggregate operator lane, not a stop-hook replacement

telemetry_contract.purpose: measure delegate emission, builder consumption, and cross-session correlation without persisting tool arguments or user query text
